title: Cyanea crispa
intro: Cyanea crispa, also known as the Hawaiian crinkled wood rose, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Cyanea crispa include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyanea crispa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Cyanea crispa is also known for its unique, hairy leaves that distinguish it from other plants in its genus.
